title: qmd
summary: QMD is a local search engine for docs/knowledge bases using BM25 + vector + LLM reranking — all on-device via node-llama-cpp with GGUF models.  The KEY insight is the context tree: 'qmd context add qmd://notes "Personal notes"' attaches hierarchical context to collections, and matching sub-documents return their parent context.
creator_view: QMD is a local search engine for docs/knowledge bases using BM25 + vector + LLM reranking — all on-device via node-llama-cpp with GGUF models. The KEY insight is the context tree: 'qmd context add qmd://notes "Personal notes"' attaches hierarchical context to collections, and matching sub-documents return their parent context. This is a retrieval pattern, not extraction, but directly relevant to T28's analysis layer — how do you search across heterogeneous extracted content? Also has MCP server with query/get/multi_get/status tools. TypeScript.
creatorLens: QMD is a local search engine for docs/knowledge bases using BM25 + vector + LLM reranking — all on-device via node-llama-cpp with GGUF models. The KEY insight is the context tree: 'qmd context add qmd://notes "Personal notes"' attaches hierarchical context to collections, and matching sub-documents return their parent context. This is a retrieval pattern, not extraction, but directly relevant to T28's analysis layer — how do you search across heterogeneous extracted content? Also has MCP server with query/get/multi_get/status tools. TypeScript.

context.t28Relevance: Context tree pattern for hierarchical retrieval across heterogeneous sources. MCP server with search tools. Local-first GGUF models. TypeScript.
notablePatterns: Context tree: hierarchical metadata attached to collections, returned with search results
Triple search: BM25 (lex) + vector (vec) + HYDE, combined via RRF + reranking
MCP server: query, get, multi_get, status tools
Collections: group different source directories with named collections
Local-only: node-llama-cpp with GGUF models, no cloud dependency
CLI-first: crwl command with --json and --files output for agent consumption
